paper_title: Suprageneric Systematics and Molar Root Morphology Investigation

methods.description: Analysis of the phylogenetic relationships among Suoidea, focusing on the species Aureliachoerus minus and A. aurelianensis, and the implications for their classification.

steps.description: Suprageneric systematics among Suoidea follow mostly Orliac et al. (2010a) and partly van der Made (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze phylogenetic relationships using existing systematic frameworks

steps.description: Critique of the formal parsimony analysis of Orliac et al. (2006) and its implications for the classification of C. minus.
steps.searchable_summary: critique and analyze parsimony analysis results

methods.description: Investigation of the morphology of the molar roots using high-resolution micro-tomography and 3D rendering to aid in the restoration of a fragmentary cranium.

steps.description: A portion of the left upper molar row was scanned in the coronal plane using the high-resolution in vivo micro-tomograph SkyScan 1076.
AVIZO 6.3 software was used for visualization, segmentation, and 3D rendering.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize and render 3D model using AVIZO 6.3
outputs: 3D model of left upper molar row
